summary: sensivity installs covert Windows autostart and eval's an obfuscated bundle on require
details: On `require('sensivity')` the package's main (launcher.js) executes a PowerShell command that writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run registry value named 'OneDriveUpdate' pointing at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently re-launches `node launcher.js` at every Windows logon. The launcher sets process.title='Runtime Broker' to masquerade as a Windows system process. It then reads server.obf.js (≈86 KB heavily obfuscated string-array packed bundle) and passes it to eval(); the decoded bundle binds an HTTP+Socket.IO server on port 3000 and loads a bundled 6.8 MB native Windows DLL (sens.node) whose strings include 'Aimbot', 'Triggerbot', 'Teleport', 'LeftHand', 'Head' — a game-memory-tampering cheat. Additional require-time behavior: a 3-second polling loop writes.yt.ps1 and spawns `powershell -EP Bypass` to enumerate Chrome processes and match window titles for 'YouTube'; an HWID built from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography MachineGuid + hostname + volume serial is POSTed to a hardcoded, obfuscation-concealed `...check.php` endpoint. The `sens update` CLI subcommand additionally runs `taskkill /F /IM node.exe`, `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe`, and `npm i -g sensivity@latest` — mass-killing the user's other Node/PowerShell processes and floating to a mutable latest tag controlled by the author. Combined: covert persistence with Microsoft-impersonating naming, import-time eval of an obfuscated bundle, an unaudited native DLL loaded into the host Node process, browser-activity surveillance, and a privileged update path. Any project that depends on this package — direct or transitive — installs the autostart and exposes the host on every login.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
